archetype: Automated High-Risk Patient Stratification and Intervention Planning System for Hospital-Acquired Sepsis Prevention
domain: Hospital Infection Control and Critical Care
description: This system identifies high-risk patients for hospital-acquired sepsis and generates personalized intervention plans to prevent sepsis onset. It is used by critical care clinicians and infection control specialists. The system must integrate with electronic health records, analyze real-time vital sign data, and comply with CDC guidelines for sepsis prevention.
rules: MUST integrate with EHR systems using HL7 FHIR standards
MUST analyze real-time vital sign data from bedside monitors
MUST apply CDC guidelines for sepsis prevention and early detection
MUST generate personalized intervention plans based on patient risk factors and medical history
MUST provide real-time alerts to clinicians for high-risk patients
anti_patterns: DO NOT rely solely on retrospective analysis of patient data — sepsis prevention requires real-time intervention
DO NOT use generic machine learning models without domain-specific feature engineering
DO NOT neglect to account for hospital-specific infection control protocols and policies
key_technical_challenges: Real-time data integration and analysis from disparate sources (EHR, bedside monitors, lab results)
Development of accurate and interpretable machine learning models for patient risk stratification
Designing user-friendly and actionable intervention plans for clinicians

recommended_stack.language: Python — for its extensive libraries and community support in healthcare and machine learning
recommended_stack.framework: Flask — for building scalable and secure web applications
recommended_stack.database_engine: PostgreSQL — for its robust support of relational data and CDC-compliant data storage
recommended_stack.cache: Redis — for caching patient data and reducing database query latency
recommended_stack.message_broker: Apache Kafka — for handling real-time data streams and alerts
